extra.reasoning: The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent). Transition [t5] on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 4) sets sent := true while opted_out is true, which directly violates the property if [t5] is reachable and can fire. It is reachable: after four ticks, ticks saturates at 4 (the cap in t4). Then optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false, with ticks remaining 4. A subsequent send_attempt has guard (opted_out and ticks>=4) = (true and 4>=4) = true, so t5 fires and sets sent := true. The resulting state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating "opted_out implies not sent". The sequence [tick, tick, tick, tick, optout, send_attempt] is 6 events, within the 8-event bound. (Although t3 with guard opted_out is also enabled on this send_attempt and would keep sent=false, t5 is an explicitly enabled transition whose update can be applied, so an execution exists in which the property is violated.)
